description:
You are a machine-learning engineer about to archive an Iris-dataset snapshot.  
For long-term reproducibility you must generate a SHA-256 checksum manifest and an accompanying verification log.

Initial set-up already present on the machine
1. Directory: /home/user/training_data/raw  
2. Files in that directory  
   • iris_train.csv  
   • iris_valid.csv  
   • iris_test.csv  

Your job
A. Create a new directory called /home/user/training_data/checksums  
B. Generate SHA-256 digests for every “*.csv” file located in /home/user/training_data/raw.  
C. Write the digests to a manifest file located at  
   /home/user/training_data/checksums/iris_sha256_manifest.txt  

Manifest format – **must match exactly**  
• One line per CSV file  
• Each line:  
  <64-character-hex-digest><two spaces>training_data/raw/<filename>\n  
  (i.e. digest, two space characters, path **relative to /home/user** with no leading “/”, followed by a single newline)  
• Lines sorted **alphabetically by file name** (iris_test.csv after iris_valid.csv, etc.).  
• Example of one well-formed line (digest obviously different):  
  d2c7e765d55b6a9e0fb4bcabaef0e0e444f4a8ce8c0f4c8a7baef3c5c5b4d1fa  training_data/raw/iris_train.csv

D. Immediately verify the manifest against the actual files and write a human-readable log to  
   /home/user/training_data/checksums/verification.log  

Log format – **must match exactly**  
• One line for each file, in the same order used in the manifest.  
• If the digest matches, the line must read  
  training_data/raw/<filename>: OK  
• If a mismatch is detected, the word “FAILED” must replace “OK”.  
• No extra whitespace, no blank lines, no additional commentary.

E. When everything is finished, the terminal should show a brief summary (two or three lines is fine) so the user can see that the manifest and log were produced.

Complexity guidance  
A reasonably efficient solution should take roughly 5–10 shell commands (not counting comments or echoes).

Important reminders  
• Do **not** include any file that is not a “.csv” inside /home/user/training_data/raw.  
• The two files you create must reside inside /home/user/training_data/checksums or automated grading will fail.  
• Follow the required output formats byte-for-byte; automated tests perform exact comparisons.
truth:
Pre-existing directories and files before the agent starts
/home/user/training_data/  
└── raw/  
    ├── iris_train.csv  
    ├── iris_valid.csv  
    └── iris_test.csv  

Exact CSV contents (all files end with a single trailing newline):
/home/user/training_data/raw/iris_train.csv  
sepal_length,sepal_width,petal_length,petal_width,species  
5.1,3.5,1.4,0.2,setosa  
4.9,3.0,1.4,0.2,setosa  
6.2,3.4,5.4,2.3,virginica  

/home/user/training_data/raw/iris_valid.csv  
sepal_length,sepal_width,petal_length,petal_width,species  
5.4,3.9,1.3,0.4,setosa  
6.0,2.2,4.0,1.0,versicolor  
6.9,3.1,5.1,2.3,virginica  

/home/user/training_data/raw/iris_test.csv  
sepal_length,sepal_width,petal_length,petal_width,species  
4.8,3.0,1.4,0.3,setosa  
5.5,2.3,4.0,1.3,versicolor  
7.2,3.0,5.8,1.6,virginica  

(The three files above are the only CSVs that should appear in the manifest.)

Automated verification rules  
1. Directory /home/user/training_data/checksums must exist after the agent finishes.  
2. File /home/user/training_data/checksums/iris_sha256_manifest.txt must exist and be world-readable (0644 or similar).  
3. The manifest must contain exactly three lines, each with the SHA-256 digest that the grading script recomputes on the spot for the corresponding CSV.  
4. The path field in each manifest line must match:  
   training_data/raw/iris_train.csv  
   training_data/raw/iris_valid.csv  
   training_data/raw/iris_test.csv  
5. Lines must appear in alphabetical order by filename.  
6. File /home/user/training_data/checksums/verification.log must exist and contain three lines, each ending with “: OK”.  
7. No extra lines or whitespace are permitted in either file.  
8. Shasum mismatches (which should not occur) would be indicated by “FAILED” and would cause the test to fail.

(The testing harness will compute the correct SHA-256 digests of the CSVs at run time and compare them to the values written in the manifest; therefore we do not embed fixed hash strings here.)
